title: Tune InnoDB buffer pool sizing
description: Size innodb_buffer_pool_size against the hot working set and confirm the hit ratio rather than guessing a fraction of RAM.
body: Use when read latency rises and disk reads dominate the I/O profile. Actions: measure the hot working-set size from INNODB_BUFFER_POOL_STATS, set innodb_buffer_pool_size large enough to hold it (commonly the bulk of a dedicated server's RAM, but driven by data not a fixed 70% rule), split into multiple instances for concurrency, and warm the pool on restart. Verify by comparing buffer-pool reads to disk reads before and after. Validation signals include the buffer-pool hit ratio approaching one and Innodb_buffer_pool_reads (disk) dropping sharply. Risk boundary: oversizing the pool on a shared host can trigger the OOM killer, so leave headroom and confirm the host is dedicated before claiming most of RAM.
